AI Interviewer Backend
  • API Reference
  • View page source

API Reference

This section covers the core modules and endpoints of the FastAPI backend and AI Agents.

Main Application

FastAPI application entrypoint.

Configures middleware, mounts routers, and manages the application lifecycle.

async app.main.health_check() → dict[source]

Health-check endpoint for monitoring and load balancing.

Returns:

JSON with status, application name, and environment.

app.main.lifespan(app: FastAPI) → AsyncGenerator[None, None][source]

Manage application startup and shutdown.

Creates database tables in development and logs lifecycle events.

Parameters:

app – The FastAPI application instance.

Yields:

Control back to the framework while the application runs.

API Endpoints

Interviews Router

Interview API routes: full interview lifecycle management.

Covers creation, file uploads, AI pipeline execution, question delivery, answer evaluation, scoring, and results retrieval.

async app.api.interviews.complete_interview(interview_id: str, current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → Result[source]

Complete an interview and generate final scores and feedback.

Runs the scoring and feedback pipelines, then stores the result.

Parameters:

  • interview_id – UUID of the interview.

  • current_user – The authenticated user.

  • db – Async database session.

Returns:

The populated result record.

async app.api.interviews.create_interview(data: InterviewCreate, current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → Interview[source]

Create a new interview session.

Parameters:

  • data – Interview settings (type, personality, difficulty).

  • current_user – The authenticated user.

  • db – Async database session.

Returns:

The newly created interview.

async app.api.interviews.delete_interview(interview_id: str, current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → None[source]

Delete an interview session.

Parameters:

  • interview_id – UUID of the interview.

  • current_user – The authenticated user.

  • db – Async database session.

async app.api.interviews.get_next_question(interview_id: str, current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → dict[str, Any][source]

Get the next interview question.

Parameters:

  • interview_id – UUID of the interview.

  • current_user – The authenticated user.

  • db – Async database session.

Returns:

The next question with its metadata.

Raises:

HTTPException – If no questions are available or all have been asked.

async app.api.interviews.get_results(interview_id: str, current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → Result[source]

Retrieve the final results for a completed interview.

Parameters:

  • interview_id – UUID of the interview.

  • current_user – The authenticated user.

  • db – Async database session.

Returns:

The result record.

Raises:

HTTPException – If results are not yet available.

async app.api.interviews.list_interviews(skip: int = 0, limit: int = 20, current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → dict[str, Any][source]

List the current user’s interview sessions.

Parameters:

  • skip – Number of records to skip (pagination offset).

  • limit – Maximum number of records to return.

  • current_user – The authenticated user.

  • db – Async database session.

Returns:

Dictionary with interviews list and total count.

async app.api.interviews.start_interview(interview_id: str, current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → Interview[source]

Start an interview by running the AI preparation pipeline.

Analyses the resume and JD, identifies skill gaps, and generates personalised interview questions.

Parameters:

  • interview_id – UUID of the interview.

  • current_user – The authenticated user.

  • db – Async database session.

Returns:

The updated interview with status questions_ready.

Raises:

HTTPException – If resume or JD text is missing.

async app.api.interviews.submit_answer(interview_id: str, data: SubmitAnswer, current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → dict[str, Any][source]

Submit and evaluate a candidate’s answer.

Appends the Q/A pair to the transcript and runs real-time evaluation.

Parameters:

  • interview_id – UUID of the interview.

  • data – The answer payload.

  • current_user – The authenticated user.

  • db – Async database session.

Returns:

Evaluation score, feedback, and optional follow-up.

async app.api.interviews.upload_jd(interview_id: str, jd_text: str | None = Form(None), file: UploadFile | None = File(None), current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → Interview[source]

Upload a job description as text or file.

Parameters:

  • interview_id – UUID of the interview.

  • jd_text – Plain-text job description.

  • file – Optional uploaded JD file.

  • current_user – The authenticated user.

  • db – Async database session.

Returns:

The updated interview.

Raises:

HTTPException – If neither text nor file is provided.

async app.api.interviews.upload_resume(interview_id: str, file: UploadFile, current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → Interview[source]

Upload a resume file for an interview session.

Saves the file and extracts its text content.

Parameters:

  • interview_id – UUID of the interview.

  • file – Uploaded resume file (PDF or TXT).

  • current_user – The authenticated user.

  • db – Async database session.

Returns:

The updated interview.

Raises:

HTTPException – If the interview is not found or doesn’t belong to the user.

Auth Router

Authentication API routes: register, login, and profile retrieval.

async app.api.auth.get_me(current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → User[source]

Return the authenticated user’s profile.

Parameters:

current_user – The user extracted from the JWT bearer token.

Returns:

The current user record.

async app.api.auth.login(data: UserLogin, db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → dict[source]

Authenticate a user and return a JWT access token.

Parameters:

  • data – Login payload with email and password.

  • db – Async database session.

Returns:

Dictionary with access_token and token_type.

Raises:

HTTPException – If the credentials are invalid.

async app.api.auth.register(data: UserRegister, db: AsyncSession = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → User[source]

Register a new user account.

Parameters:

  • data – Registration payload with email, password, and optional name.

  • db – Async database session.

Returns:

The newly created user record.

Raises:

HTTPException – If the email is already registered.

Media & WebRTC Router

Media API routes: STT, TTS, and Avatar streaming endpoints.

Provides WebSockets and standard HTTP endpoints for Voice/Video interactions.

class app.api.media.AvatarSessionRequest(*, personality_mode: str = 'professional')[source]

Bases: BaseModel

model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

personality_mode: str
class app.api.media.AvatarSpeakRequest(*, conversation_id: str, text: str)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

conversation_id: str
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

text: str
async app.api.media.avatar_speak(request: AvatarSpeakRequest)[source]

Send text directly to an active Tavus conversation for the avatar to speak.

async app.api.media.create_avatar_session(request: AvatarSessionRequest)[source]

Initialize a Tavus conversational video WebRTC session. Returns {conversation_id, conversation_url}.

async app.api.media.speech_to_text(file: UploadFile = File(PydanticUndefined))[source]

Convert uploaded audio file to text.

async app.api.media.text_to_speech(text: str)[source]

Convert text to speech audio. (Useful for fallback HTTP pooling)

async app.api.media.websocket_media_stream(websocket: WebSocket)[source]

Bidirectional WebSocket for real-time STT and Interviewer responses.

Accepts audio chunks from the client, transcribes them, and eventually can return the AI’s spoken response (TTS audio) directly through the socket.

Code Sandbox Router

class app.api.code_execution.CodeExecutionRequest(*, language: str, version: str = '*', code: str)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

code: str
language: str
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

version: str
class app.api.code_execution.CodeExecutionResponse(*, stdout: str, stderr: str, compile_output: str | None = None, language: str, version: str)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

compile_output: str | None
language: str
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

stderr: str
stdout: str
version: str
async app.api.code_execution.execute_code(request: CodeExecutionRequest)[source]

Executes the given code securely by proxying to the public Piston API. This acts as a remote code sandbox for the technical/DSA rounds.

AI Agents (LangGraph)

Interviewer Agent

Interviewer Agent.

Evaluates candidate answers in real-time during the interview, providing scores, feedback, and follow-up questions.

class agents.interviewer_agent.InterviewerAgent[source]

Bases: BaseAgent

Agent that evaluates individual answers during a live interview.

Provides per-answer scoring, qualitative feedback, strength/weakness identification, and optional follow-up questions.

async evaluate_answer(question_text: str, answer_text: str, personality_mode: str = 'professional', jd_context: str = '') → dict[source]

Evaluate a single candidate answer.

Parameters:

  • question_text – The interview question that was asked.

  • answer_text – The candidate’s answer.

  • personality_mode – Interviewer style for the evaluation tone.

  • jd_context – Job description context for relevance scoring.

Returns:

Dictionary with score, feedback, follow_up, etc.

async run(state: dict) → dict[source]

No-op for pipeline mode — use evaluate_answer instead.

Parameters:

state – Pipeline state (unused).

Returns:

The unmodified state.

async agents.interviewer_agent.evaluate_single_answer(question_text: str, answer_text: str, jd_context: str = '', personality_mode: str = 'professional') → dict[source]

Evaluate a single answer — convenience function for API routes.

Parameters:

  • question_text – The interview question.

  • answer_text – The candidate’s answer.

  • jd_context – Job description context.

  • personality_mode – Interviewer personality style.

Returns:

Dictionary with score, feedback, and follow_up.

LangGraph Orchestrator

LangGraph Orchestrator.

Wires all agents into a pipeline for the AI interview workflow. Provides two main pipelines:

  1. Preparation: resume → jd → skill_gap → questions

  2. Scoring: evaluate answers → generate feedback

class agents.orchestrator.InterviewState[source]

Bases: TypedDict

Shared state flowing through the agent pipeline.

difficulty_level: str
dsa_problems: list
error: str | None
feedback_result: dict
interview_type: str
jd_analysis: dict
jd_text: str
personality_mode: str
questions: list
resume_analysis: dict
resume_text: str
scoring_result: dict
skill_gap_report: dict
transcript: list
agents.orchestrator.build_langgraph_workflow() → Any[source]

Build a LangGraph StateGraph for advanced pipeline usage.

Returns:

The compiled LangGraph workflow, or None if LangGraph is not installed.

async agents.orchestrator.run_preparation_pipeline(resume_text: str, jd_text: str, interview_type: str = 'full', personality_mode: str = 'professional', difficulty: str = 'medium') → dict[source]

Run the interview preparation pipeline.

Executes sequentially: Resume Analysis → JD Analysis → Skill Gap Detection → Question Generation.

Parameters:

  • resume_text – Extracted text from the candidate’s resume.

  • jd_text – Job description text.

  • interview_type – Type of interview (full, technical, behavioral, dsa).

  • personality_mode – Interviewer personality setting.

  • difficulty – Question difficulty level.

Returns:

The complete pipeline state with all analysis results.

Raises:

RuntimeError – If any pipeline step fails.

async agents.orchestrator.run_scoring_pipeline(transcript: list | str, questions: list | str, jd_text: str = '', resume_analysis: dict | str | None = None) → dict[source]

Run the scoring and feedback pipeline.

Executes sequentially: Scoring → Feedback Generation.

Parameters:

  • transcript – List of Q/A pairs (or JSON string).

  • questions – List of questions (or JSON string).

  • jd_text – Job description text for context.

  • resume_analysis – Parsed resume analysis (or JSON string).

Returns:

Dictionary with scores and feedback sub-dicts.

Resume Parsing Agent

Resume Analyzer Agent.

Extracts a structured candidate profile from resume text using the LLM.

class agents.resume_agent.ResumeAgent[source]

Bases: BaseAgent

Agent that analyses resume text and extracts a structured profile.

Produces a dictionary containing skills, experience, projects, education, and professional strengths.

async run(state: dict) → dict[source]

Analyse resume text and enrich the pipeline state.

Parameters:

state – Must contain resume_text (str).

Returns:

The state updated with resume_analysis (dict).

async agents.resume_agent.analyze_resume(state: dict) → dict[source]

Module-level convenience function for the orchestrator.

Parameters:

state – Pipeline state with resume_text.

Returns:

Updated state with resume_analysis.

Core Services

Speech & Audio Service

class app.services.speech_service.SpeechService[source]

Bases: object

Service for handling Text-to-Speech (TTS) and Speech-to-Text (STT).

Uses Deepgram for both STT (transcription) and TTS (Aura models).

async classmethod speech_to_text(audio_bytes: bytes, filename: str = 'audio.wav') → str[source]

Convert spoken audio back to text using Deepgram STT.

Parameters:

  • audio_bytes – The raw audio data.

  • filename – The filename (useful for extensions).

Returns:

The transcribed text.

async classmethod text_to_speech(text: str, voice_id: str = 'aura-asteria-en', personality_mode: str = 'professional') → bytes[source]

Convert conversational text to spoken audio using Deepgram TTS.

Parameters:

  • text – The text to convert to speech.

  • voice_id – The default Deepgram Aura voice profile (overridden by personality_mode if set).

  • personality_mode – The tone of the interviewer (friendly, strict, professional).

Returns:

The raw audio bytes (e.g., MP3 or WAV format).

Avatar (WebRTC) Service

class app.services.avatar_service.AvatarService[source]

Bases: object

async classmethod create_conversation(personality_mode: str = 'professional') → dict[source]

Create a new Tavus WebRTC conversation. Returns the conversation_id, conversation_url (WebRTC endpoint).

async classmethod send_message(conversation_id: str, text: str) → dict[source]

Make the active Tavus avatar speak the text asynchronously.

Database Models
